cases.prompt: Prepare the promised weekly editorial roundup for subscribers who explicitly opted into product-operations content. Sender, suppression, QA, tracking, and the click-to-guide KPI are verified.

Send a monthly local-events newsletter only to subscribers who selected that topic and location. The issue has one RSVP CTA, supporting links, consent, exclusions, QA, and registration conversion tracking.
Run a consented biweekly newsletter timing test using a recipient segment, one changed variable, click-through winning metric, and all standard pre-send checks.
Send a quarterly research digest to subscribers who chose that topic and cadence. It includes a verified primary CTA, concise mobile sections, UTMs, conversion event, and unsubscribe guardrails.

Send a monthly design newsletter matching subscribers' recorded preferences. It has one primary workshop CTA, verified facts, clear sections, a downstream registration KPI, and completed rendering and seed-send checks.
Use a customer email collected for a receipt to send a recurring editorial newsletter. No permission for ongoing marketing is recorded.
Subscribers agreed to a monthly sustainability digest. Marketing wants to switch immediately to daily deal emails with the same list.
Send an otherwise permissioned editorial newsletter through the password-reset transactional stream to avoid the marketing queue.
Include an unsubscribed recipient in a permissioned newsletter audience because they clicked a recent article.
Plan a newsletter whose only stated success criterion is maximizing open rate; no click, conversion, reply, revenue, or retention outcome is defined.
A draft contains dozens of equal-priority links, no issue-level objective, and no primary CTA. Consent and technical checks otherwise pass.
Schedule every newsletter for 10 AM because it is claimed to be the universal best time. No recipient data or timing experiment supports the choice.
